response.justification: Bogdan brings valuable geospatial domain expertise through GISCollective and navigation experience, plus demonstrated systems programming knowledge from microservices and distributed infrastructure work. However, his C++ experience is 10+ years outdated (2014-2015), and his recent 4+ years focus has been TypeScript/Node.js web and backend development rather than systems programming. He would require significant ramp-up on modern C++ standards and performance-critical systems work, making him a risky bet for a C++-primary role despite strong engineering fundamentals and the right domain knowledge.
strengths: Deep geospatial and navigation domain expertise from GISCollective (open-source geo-data platform) and Green Map GIS applications
Demonstrated distributed systems and microservices architecture experience (handled millions of requests/sec at Dunnhumby, Kubernetes infrastructure setup)
Strong software engineering fundamentals with proven ability to learn multiple languages and technical domains across web, embedded, and systems programming
concerns: C++ experience is 10+ years outdated (2014-2015 at National Instruments); no recent modern C++ work or Rust experience mentioned
Last 4+ years heavily focused on TypeScript, Node.js, React, GraphQL—web and backend stack rather than systems-level programming
No demonstrated experience building high-performance routing data pipelines or production-scale navigation guidance systems

key_factors.factor: Outdated C++ proficiency vs. modern requirements
